options.note: WEEKDAY-PRIORITIZED long options (calls/puts only, no selling premium). Augments equities/ETFs; crypto stays 24/7. Premium can decay to $0, so exits are STRICT: +100% target / -50% stop / force-close at <=1 DTE. Caps are premium-at-risk (qty x price x 100).

notes: Process goal: maximize risk-adjusted paper P&L while keeping drawdown < 6%, cash reserve > 5%, and all caps enforced. Aspirational target 10x/30d is secondary to never breaking a hard rule. Equity positions close 1:45 PM MT. Crypto 24/7. Regime filter applies.

risk.note: Code-enforced hard constraints (scripts/trade.py validate_order). Cash, allocation, concentration, and position-count caps stay enforced. Daily soft/hard stops are advisory only while connected to Alpaca paper trading.

validation_mode.note: Active automatically until completed_trades_threshold trades are logged. Overrides risk caps with tighter limits to protect small account during strategy validation.

research_mode.note: PAPER-ONLY mode. Relaxes the loss-streak lockout + validation caps so the agent can run its swing strategy freely, but it is QUALITY-GATED: high min_buy_score, concentrated book, and NO forced auto-buys (forcing trades was -EV). All safeguard code is retained — set enabled=false (or point APCA_BASE_URL at live) to restore every guardrail. Toggle from the dashboard. Cash-reserve, limit-only orders, and stops are NEVER relaxed.

trading_style.note: SWING / position trading — the agent holds multi-day while the thesis and trend are intact; there is NO forced intraday flatten. Quality over quantity: enter only momentum-CONFIRMED setups (released-bullish squeeze, MACD bullish, reclaiming VWAP, volume), let winners run with a trailing stop, and cut losers fast. This is the strategy that makes aggressive compounding ($100->$1,000) even possible; the old day-trade churn was -3%/trade.
trading_style.profile: aggressive — cut losers fast at -3% (unchanged); let winners run: trail still arms at +5% to protect gains, but partial is taken later (+10%) and the trail gives back 4% from peak (vs 3%) to ride bigger trends

live_account.note: When enabled, paper orders are scaled to the real small account so behavior mirrors the live $100 -> $1,000 challenge. When disabled, paper sizes on full equity but prompts still factor the small-account reality.

api_config.note: Model routing by routine. Opus for high-stakes decisions; Sonnet for intraday cycles; Haiku for rule-based scoring and news summarization. max_tokens caps only limit UNUSED output (you pay per generated token, not per cap) — they MUST stay high enough to fit the full structured-JSON decision for every watched/held symbol or the payload truncates mid-object and extraction fails (no orders, no alerts). Decision routines (trading/cycle/crypto) need room for many symbols + reasoning.

notifications.note: Discord alerting. proposal_alerts posts the agent's intended decision each cycle (independent of execution). decision_alerts posts the executed OUTCOME, but only when something actually filled (so it doesn't duplicate the proposal on idle cycles). Set proposal_alerts_when_idle=true to also post stand-pat (all hold/skip) cycles.

discord.note: 10-channel operator routing ported from FeintTrade. Channel IDs live in .env (DISCORD_CH_*); posting uses DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN via the bot API and falls back to command_post then the DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL. Severity cooldowns + dedup prevent alert spam; trades/proposals are INFO (cooldown 0) so they always post. Set multichannel_enabled=false to revert to single-webhook behavior.

penny_risk.note: Manipulation/liquidity guards for low-priced discovered names (ported from FeintTrade). A cheap stock spiking hard on the day is pump-and-dump-prone; thin share volume at a low price is untrustworthy.

discovery.note: Marketwide scanner (scripts/screener.py) surfaces trending tradable Alpaca symbols beyond the static watchlist.

dynamic_watchlist.note: Auto-updating watchlist (scripts/watchlist_manager.py). Recurring high-quality discovery candidates are auto-PROMOTED into a persisted dynamic list (data/dynamic_watchlist.json) and injected into the decision prompts; stale ones are demoted. Never rewrites this file. Promotions/demotions post to #ft-watchlist. Penny-caution names are never promoted.

watchlist.description: 3x Nasdaq-100 (ProShares) — primary bull momentum vehicle
3x PHLX Semiconductor (Direxion) — AI/chip momentum
3x FANG+ (MicroSectors) — mega-cap tech concentrated exposure
3x Biotech (Direxion) — ultra-volatile, catalyst-driven
3x Financials (Direxion) — diversifies beyond tech; financials sector leadership vehicle
3x inverse Semiconductors (Direxion) — momentum LONG that profits when semis/tech fall; tradeable in ANY regime on its own confirmed bullish momentum (it rises as the market drops)
3x inverse Nasdaq-100 — momentum LONG that profits when the Nasdaq falls; tradeable in ANY regime on its own confirmed bullish momentum (distribution days, rotations)
1.5x VIX futures — panic hedge only, hold minutes-hours max
NVIDIA — AI chip leader, gap-and-go + breakout plays
Advanced Micro Devices — secondary semi play, pairs with NVDA
Tesla — extreme vol, gap-and-go, news-driven momentum
MicroStrategy — Bitcoin proxy, correlated to BTC moves
Coinbase — crypto exchange proxy, moves with BTC/ETH
Palantir — AI/defense, momentum + breakout tendencies
Bitcoin — primary crypto, 24/7 scored system
Ethereum — smart contract leader, high liquidity
Solana — high-speed L1, high beta vs BTC
Dogecoin — meme momentum, social-driven spikes
Avalanche — DeFi/L1, explosive alt momentum
Chainlink — oracle/AI narrative, strong mid-cap momentum
XRP — high liquidity, news-sensitive regulatory plays

options_watchlist.note: Long calls/puts only. Buy on high-conviction breakout; exit +100%/-50%/<=1 DTE.
Most liquid options in the market — tight spreads, broad-market directional.
